en_title: Crossfire Is Being Built by One of Gaming’s Best Design Duos
cn_title: 《穿越火线》值得关注，幕后是两位顶级设计师
subtitle: 枪火背后的巧手
opus_summary: 《穿越火线》新作由Taylor Kurosaki与Jacob Minkoff领衔。两人曾打造《神秘海域》经典场面和《使命召唤：现代战争》重启，IGN作者认为他们能把设计与叙事再次拧在一起。

paragraphs.en: This is an IGN opinion piece from writer Jeremy Peel, who has played (and occassionally reviewed) every single Call of Duty campaign. He particularly regrets Black Ops 3.
paragraphs.cn: 本文是IGN作者Jeremy Peel的一篇观点文章。他玩过（偶尔也评测过）每一部《使命召唤》战役。他尤其后悔玩过《黑色行动3》。

paragraphs.en: “For over 18 years, Jacob and I have developed character-driven games,” said Taylor Kurosaki during the Summer Games Fest reveal of Crossfire , “with the goal of thoroughly connecting design and story.”
paragraphs.cn: Taylor Kurosaki在夏日游戏节公布《穿越火线》时说：「十八年多来，Jacob和我一直在开发角色驱动的游戏，目标是让设计与故事彻底连接起来。」

paragraphs.en: As he said “design”, Kurosaki let his hand rest briefly on the shoulder of his creative partner, Jacob Minkoff, resplendent in a yellow bow-tie and handlebar moustache. And as he said “story”, he let that hand drift back to the long lapels of his own suit jacket. It was a quiet demonstration of each man’s expertise; a balance of elements that has shaped a number of brilliant blockbuster games.
paragraphs.cn: 当他说到「设计」时，Kurosaki把手短暂地搭在创意搭档Jacob Minkoff的肩上，后者系着黄色领结，留着漂亮的八字胡。当他说到「故事」时，他又让那只手回到自己西装外套的长翻领上。这是一次安静的示范，展示了两人各自的专长，也展示了一种塑造过多部杰出大片游戏的元素平衡。

paragraphs.en: Both men honed their crafts at Naughty Dog, where Kurosaki worked for more than 10 years as a narrative design lead. Minkoff, meanwhile, played a part in bringing Uncharted 2’s beloved train level to life – before designing the cruise ship escape of Uncharted 3: Drake’s Deception. There, you react to the shifting weight of the listing vessel as it floods with water, tips on its side, and begins to sink. Fighting your way below decks from the ballroom, you’re forced to adjust to the gradually-altered reality of the ship’s interior, as chandeliers swing and luxury cars slide across the hold.
paragraphs.cn: 两人都曾在顽皮狗磨炼技艺。Kurosaki在那里担任叙事设计主管超过10年。Minkoff则参与打造了《神秘海域2》中深受喜爱的火车关卡，后来又设计了《神秘海域3：德雷克的诡计》中的邮轮逃生段落。在那里，你会随着船体进水、侧倾并开始下沉，对不断变化的重量做出反应。从舞厅一路打到甲板下方时，你被迫适应船舱内部逐渐改变的现实：吊灯摇摆，豪车在货舱里滑动。

paragraphs.en: This has become something of a signature for Minkoff-Kurosaki design ever since. Not cruise ships, precisely, but a reconfiguration of the way the player interacts with a level – turning what is usually static into something fluid. In Crossfire, that means navigating a craggy mountainside in a different way than you’re used to, thanks to the game’s adaptive cover system. “Our player character Layla dynamically adjusts her stance to react to the complex terrain and the enemy’s line of sight,” Minkoff said at SGF. “It fundamentally changes your relationship with the environment and becomes an active skill to master.”
paragraphs.cn: 此后，这几乎成了Minkoff-Kurosaki式设计的标志。不一定是邮轮本身，而是重新配置玩家与关卡互动的方式，把通常静止的东西变成流动的东西。在《穿越火线》中，这意味着借助游戏的自适应掩体系统，以不同于以往的方式穿越崎岖山坡。Minkoff在SGF上说：「我们的玩家角色Layla会动态调整姿态，以应对复杂地形和敌人的视线。它从根本上改变了你和环境之间的关系，并成为一种需要掌握的主动技能。」

paragraphs.en: Kurosaki and Minkoff left Naughty Dog not long after Amy Hennig’s departure, during a creative shakeup that left the directors of The Last of Us as the studio’s new figureheads. And the pair joined Infinity Ward – a 25 minute drive down the road – at a time when the Call of Duty developer had been in the wilderness. Infinity Ward’s founders had left in acrimonious circumstances a few years prior, taking many talented team members with them to found Titanfall’s Respawn Entertainment. 2013’s Call of Duty: Ghosts had felt narratively muddled as a result – indebted to the past and timid when stepping toward the future.
paragraphs.cn: Kurosaki和Minkoff是在Amy Hennig离开后不久离开顽皮狗的。当时的创意洗牌让《最后生还者》的导演们成为工作室新的领军人物。两人随后加入了Infinity Ward，车程只有25分钟，而当时这家《使命召唤》开发商正处在迷失期。Infinity Ward的创始人几年前在激烈冲突中离开，并带走了许多有才华的成员，创立了《泰坦陨落》开发商Respawn Entertainment。受此影响，2013年的《使命召唤：幽灵》在叙事上显得混乱，既欠着过去的债，又在迈向未来时畏首畏尾。

paragraphs.en: 2016’s Infinite Warfare – on which Kurosaki and Minkoff worked as narrative director and design director respectively – felt very different indeed. While some COD fans couldn’t countenance the idea of a story set in space, Infinity Ward grounded its campaign in deliberately-paced character drama, just as Naughty Dog had for the preceding decade. The studio learned the right lessons from Wolfenstein: The New Order, too: not only that it should cast B.J. Blazkowicz actor Brian Bloom in the lead role, but that it should situate the heart of its FPS in a conversationally-focused hub world.
paragraphs.cn: 2016年的《无限战争》感觉完全不同。Kurosaki和Minkoff分别担任该作叙事总监和设计总监。虽然一些COD粉丝无法接受故事设定在太空，但Infinity Ward让战役扎根于刻意放慢节奏的角色戏剧，就像顽皮狗此前十年所做的那样。工作室也从《德军总部：新秩序》中学到了正确的经验：不仅应该让B.J. Blazkowicz的演员Brian Bloom担任主角，还应该把这款第一人称射击游戏的核心放在一个以对话为中心的枢纽世界里。

paragraphs.en: In Infinite Warfare, that was the UNSA Retribution – a warship that your protagonist, Nick Reyes, first storms onto in a state of heightened (if manfully contained) anguish. The ship’s captain has steered the Retribution into the path of an enemy supercarrier in a desperate attempt to buy Earth’s defenders time – killing and injuring many of his own crew in the process. As the survivors rush to put out the flames, Reyes seeks to confront the captain, and remind him that a commander’s job is to bring those under his command safely home. But as it transpires, the captain is dead; Reyes is promoted to take his place, and finds himself standing among the personal effects of a man he’ll never get to hold to account, nor demand an explanation from.
paragraphs.cn: 在《无限战争》中，这个枢纽是UNSA Retribution，一艘战舰。你的主角Nick Reyes第一次冲上这艘舰时，正处在高涨但强行压抑的痛苦之中。舰长为了给地球守军争取时间，绝望地把Retribution驶向敌方超级航母的路径，过程中导致大量己方船员死伤。幸存者忙着灭火时，Reyes试图去质问舰长，提醒他指挥官的职责是把部下安全带回家。但事实证明舰长已经死亡；Reyes被提升为接替他的位置，并发现自己站在一个他永远无法追责、也无法要求解释的人留下的私人物品之间。

paragraphs.en: Infinite Warfare’s woozy atmosphere is that of a neverending overtime shift, as you rush to punish your enemies for the shock destruction of most of Earth’s military fleet. And its journey is one in which you get to know the remaining crew of the Retribution face to face – a clashing mix of blue-collar stoicism, wisecracking AI and cockney bravura, the latter from a brilliant David Harewood. By the end, you find yourself making the same decision as that captain: sacrificing your crew, who are finally all pulling in the same direction, for the people they’ve pledged to defend. It’s the kind of confident thematic throughline that had been unimaginable in Call of Duty: Ghosts.
paragraphs.cn: 《无限战争》的恍惚氛围像是一场永无止境的加班。你急着惩罚敌人，因为他们突然摧毁了地球大部分军事舰队。而它的旅程，则让你面对面认识Retribution上剩下的船员：蓝领式坚忍、爱讲俏皮话的AI，以及来自出色演员David Harewood的伦敦腔豪气相互碰撞。到最后，你会发现自己做出了和那位舰长一样的决定：牺牲终于同心协力的船员，去保护他们曾宣誓守护的人。这种自信的主题线索，在《使命召唤：幽灵》中曾是不可想象的。

paragraphs.en: Meanwhile, the design approach that had birthed Uncharted 3’s cruise ship freed Infinite Warfare from the very concepts of up and down, left and right. Leaping from the spinning cockpit of your fighter jet, you might grapple with a Martian soldier in open space. Then find footing on the angular exterior hull of a battleship, tilting your view to 45 degrees. Before finally heading in through an airlock, where gravity as we know it is restored. In this mode, COD felt giddy and delirious.
paragraphs.cn: 与此同时，孕育出《神秘海域3》邮轮关卡的设计方法，让《无限战争》摆脱了上下左右这些概念。从旋转战机座舱中跃出后，你可能会在开放太空中与一名火星士兵缠斗。随后你在一艘战舰带棱角的外壳上找到立足点，把视角倾斜到45度。最后你穿过气闸进入内部，我们熟悉的重力又恢复了。在这种模式下，COD变得眩目又迷离。

paragraphs.en: Afterwards, Minkoff and Kurosaki pulled off the 2019 reboot of Call of Duty: Modern Warfare – a coup that many have credited with the rejuvenation of the series during the pandemic. Warzone made most of the headlines, but the campaign continued the exceptional character work Infinite Warfare’s directors had established, rejecting bombast in favour of intimacy and tension. Of particular note was the story of Farah and her brother Hadir – beginning in traumatic childhood as you snuck around during a home invasion, evading a Russian soldier and stabbing at him with a screwdriver. “You survive,” says her dying father, squeezing his last breaths from faltering lungs. “Whatever it takes, never back down.”
paragraphs.cn: 此后，Minkoff和Kurosaki完成了2019年的《使命召唤：现代战争》重启。许多人认为这次成功让系列在疫情期间重新焕发生机。《战区》占据了大多数头条，但战役延续了《无限战争》导演们确立的出色角色塑造，拒绝浮夸，转向亲密感和紧张感。尤其值得注意的是Farah和她哥哥Hadir的故事：它从创伤性的童年开始，你在一次入室袭击中潜行，躲避一名俄罗斯士兵，并用螺丝刀刺向他。「活下去，」她垂死的父亲用逐渐衰弱的肺挤出最后的气息说，「不惜一切，永不退缩。」

paragraphs.en: Farah and Hadir’s divergent interpretations of this final command fuels the action of the rest of the campaign – the two siblings winding up on opposite sides of the American definition of rebel and terrorist. The pain of that split is carried emotionally by Claudia Doumit, best known for her turn as Victoria Neuman in The Boys. And now Doumit has returned to take the lead role in Crossfire – a continuity in casting that suggests Kurosaki and Minkoff might meet their narrative ambitions once again.
paragraphs.cn: Farah和Hadir对这句临终命令截然不同的理解，推动了战役后续的行动。这对兄妹最终站在美国对反叛者与恐怖分子的定义两侧。二人分裂带来的痛苦，由Claudia Doumit在情感上承载。她最为人熟知的角色，是《黑袍纠察队》中的Victoria Neuman。现在Doumit回归并在《穿越火线》中担任主角，这种选角上的连续性暗示Kurosaki和Minkoff或许能够再次实现他们的叙事野心。

paragraphs.en: “In Crossfire, two opposing operators must form a temporary alliance,” Kurosaki said onstage at SGF. “Depending on one another is their only chance of survival.” This setup is a reminder, too, of how Naughty Dog made its name in storytelling: by exploring inharmonious relationships under pressure. Think of Nathan Drake and Elena Fisher; Nathan Drake and Chloe Frazer; Nathan Drake and Victor Sullivan. It’s by pairing these personalities off and lighting a fire beneath them that Naughty Dog achieved magic. And I suspect that Minkoff and Kurosaki, applying all they’ve learned, can achieve the same in Crossfire.
paragraphs.cn: Kurosaki在SGF台上说：「在《穿越火线》中，两名敌对干员必须结成临时联盟。相互依赖是他们唯一的生存机会。」这个设定也让人想起顽皮狗如何通过讲故事打出名声：探索压力之下并不和谐的人际关系。想想Nathan Drake和Elena Fisher，Nathan Drake和Chloe Frazer，Nathan Drake和Victor Sullivan。正是把这些性格配成一对，再在他们脚下点火，顽皮狗才创造了魔法。而我怀疑，Minkoff和Kurosaki把他们学到的一切用在《穿越火线》上，也能做到同样的事。

paragraphs.en: Jeremy Peel is a freelance journalist and friend to anyone who will look at photos of his dogs.
paragraphs.cn: Jeremy Peel是一名自由记者，也是任何愿意看他家狗照片之人的朋友。
